title: Boldus boldus

Further research on the specific classification of Boldus boldus is required for a more detailed understanding.

intro: Boldus boldus, also known as Boldo or Peumus boldus, is a South American shrub native to Chile and Argentina, belonging to the family Grossulariaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and its health benefits include digestive relief, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects, making it a natural remedy for various gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Boldus boldus include boldine, a potent alkaloid with hepatoprotective and anti-inflammatory properties, as well as other compounds such as flavonoids and terpenoids. In traditional herbal medicine, Boldus boldus is commonly prepared as an infusion, tea, or decoction to treat digestive disorders, and its extracts are also used in modern pharmaceutical formulations to support liver health and prevent liver damage.
